sessions.intent: Validate test quality for collections-are-data v3 (dict/list native Data, one compare path, list/dict ops).

before.assumptions: codeanalyzer v2 PASS, C# 4082/0 + plang 273/273 claimed. coder env note: sandbox build LLM mis-compiles, plang .pr built elsewhere.
before.risk: Branch rewrites the compare path (review-driven, highest false-green risk) and adds native collection types; new where action; two comparison-operator .pr were rebuilt by the bad LLM and committed.

actions.context: Read Stage1-6 C# tests. Stage4 (compare) and Stage5 (list/dict ops) verify intent: numeric-vs-lexical order, trichotomy, structural equality, equality-only types throw on Order, where/group/unique behavior incl. edge cases (WhereOnApex/SortOnListOfDict throw).
Mutation test: flipped text-order arm OrdinalIgnoreCase->Ordinal; Stage4 Compare_TextCaseInsensitive_OrderAndEqualsAgree failed as expected; reverted. Compare-path green is real, not false.

Two called-sub-goal .pr were rebuilt by the bad sandbox LLM and committed (builderVersion null, path collapsed to /WhenLess.goal). The actual test .pr (conditionlessthan.test.pr) is unchanged and still exercises the < operator. Process smell, not a test-integrity break.
where is a NEW action (Stage5) with C# coverage (WhereOnList/Dict/Apex) but no plang .test.goal. Builder step->action mapping unverified; builder env (bad LLM) blocks closing it here.

after.health: C# 4082/4082, plang 273/273, both deterministic, git clean across two runs.
after.notes: Findings are minor: missing plang test for new where action; bad-LLM .pr rebuilds committed; missing coder baseline-tests.md. None are red tests or confirmed false-greens.

sessions.intent: Validate test quality for collections-are-data v6; confirm scope of the F2 disabled signing tests as codeanalyzer v4 requested.

before.assumptions: codeanalyzer v4 PASS (C# 4089/0 + plang 273/273); F2 = two signing tests disabled as a documented, merge-gated deferral. Asked tester to confirm scope + that the C# probe still covers the primitive.
before.risk: Disabled tests are a classic false-green vector; the branch reworks the signing/Data-wrapping path.

actions.context: Both were green regression tests at base (verify a signed value through %list[0]% and across a goal call). On this branch they were gutted: real steps commented out, replaced with a no-op `write out '...disabled'` that PASSES and counts in plang 273/273 (0 skipped). Masks a real regression (Data-wrapping-Data rehash mismatch).
REPRO: restored the un-gutted goal + base .pr, ran on the current branch binary -> [Fail] (200ms). Proves verify-signed-through-list is broken now, not just per the comment. Reverted; git clean.

The cited C# probe passes only because it reads the element via raw (IList)[0] as data cast and verifies the bare element — bypasses the broken plang %list[0]% / goal-call surface. So broken developer behavior has zero surface coverage.

F1 aliasing fix independently mutation-verified: CopyStructure -> return this reds Add_List_DoesNotAliasSourceVariable + AddList_StructureCopy_NoAliasBothDirections and only those. Reverted. Honest fix.

after.health: C# 4089/4089 genuine. plang 273/273 is a FALSE GREEN: 2 gutted no-op tests masking a live regression (verify of signed value across list/goal boundary).
after.notes: Blocker = finding 1 (masked regression). Signing fix legitimately deferred to signature-as-schema-wrapper + merge gate; on-branch fix is small: make the two goals Skipped (tag exclude) instead of no-op passes. F1/F3/F4 genuinely resolved.

sessions.intent: Re-review coder v7, which resolves the v6 false-green by making the 2 signing tests honestly Skipped.

before.assumptions: Coder v7 replaced the no-op-pass disable with a source-text `skip` tag (HasSkipTag) -> Status.Skipped; claims 271 pass + 2 skipped + 0 fail.
before.risk: New discovery mechanism (regex on goal source) is itself a potential false-green vector if it over-matches and silently skips real tests.

actions.context: Both goals now hold their REAL steps again (sign -> store/goal-call/list -> verify -> assert) with a leading `- tag this test 'skip'` + comment. Parked, not gutted; re-enable = delete the tag line. Confirmed both register [Skipped], not Pass.

HasSkipTag: anchored regex ^\s*tag\s+this\s+test\s+['"]skip['"]\s*$ on goal source steps, short-circuits to Skipped before build/.pr check. Over-match check: a `tag this test 'http'`/`'fast','slow'` goal still RUNS+PASSES (live proof, not skipped). No C# regression test for HasSkipTag itself -> minor finding 1.

plang suite: 271 pass + 2 skipped + 0 fail, deterministic across isolated runs, git clean. One flaky timeout on a timing-sensitive enforce-timeout test while C# suite ran concurrently; cleared on every isolated run (CPU contention, not a branch bug). C# 4089/0.

after.health: C# 4089/4089; plang 271 pass + 2 skipped + 0 fail (honest count, was a false 273/273 in v6). Deterministic, git clean.
after.notes: v6 blocker resolved and verified (not just asserted). Signing regression genuinely still deferred to signature-as-schema-wrapper, merge gate intact. Minor findings: HasSkipTag lacks a regression test (over-match = future false-green vector, empirically safe today); `where` still lacks a plang test.
